summary: Completed comprehensive testing of FitAI V3 features: full-body photo validation, shoes upload section, and swipe page enhancements. All backend APIs working correctly. Frontend validation flow working as expected.

issues: Clothing items badge uses white background while shoes use dusty-rose - this is intentional design differentiation per requirements

passed_tests: Backend API /api/ returns correct FitAI message
Backend API /api/validate-person-photo endpoint exists and returns 200
Backend API /api/validate-person-photo returns required fields: valid, feedback
Backend API /api/generate-outfits accepts num_clothing and num_shoes form fields
Backend API /api/generate-outfits requires minimum 3 clothing items (returns 400 for <3)
Backend API /api/generate-outfits rejects more than 8 items (returns 400 for >8)
Backend API /api/generate-outfit-image endpoint exists and returns success field
Backend API /api/saved-looks GET returns array correctly
Backend API /api/saved-looks POST creates and persists saved look
Backend API /api/saved-looks DELETE removes saved look correctly
Frontend landing page loads with FitAI branding and Try it now button
Frontend onboarding Step 1 shows 'Full body required' text
Frontend onboarding Step 1 Next button disabled until valid photo uploaded
Frontend photo validation correctly rejects non-person images with error message
Frontend photo validation clears invalid photo and shows feedback
Frontend SwipePage correctly redirects to onboarding when no outfit data
Frontend Saved Looks page shows empty state with Start styling button
critical_code_review_comments: Code quality is good - all new features properly implemented
Photo validation API correctly returns structured response with valid, is_full_body, is_person_visible, feedback fields
Shoes section in Step 2 has distinct dusty-rose badge (bg-[#C9908A]/90) vs white for clothing
SwipePage card height correctly set to 82vh with max-h-[800px]
Auto-generation useEffect properly checks autoGenerate flag before triggering
Dots indicator correctly shows dusty-rose for generated images, sage green for current
seed_data_creation: None required - tests create and cleanup their own data
context_for_next_testing_agent: All V3 features tested and working: (1) Full-body photo validation API works correctly - rejects non-person images and clears photo on failure, (2) generate-outfits accepts num_clothing and num_shoes fields with proper validation (min 3, max 8), (3) Shoes section in onboarding Step 2 has distinct dusty-rose badge, (4) SwipePage has 82vh card height and auto-generation logic. Note: Full end-to-end flow testing with real person photos requires manual testing due to AI validation.

full_body_validation.notes: Correctly rejects non-person images, returns structured feedback

shoes_upload_section.notes: Max 3 shoes, optional, dusty-rose badge (bg-[#C9908A]/90), Footprints icon

generate_outfits_with_shoes.notes: Accepts num_clothing and num_shoes, validates min 3 and max 8 items

swipe_card_size.notes: h-[82vh] max-h-[800px] for bigger full-body view

auto_generation.notes: Triggers when autoGenerate flag is true, generates sequentially

loading_state.notes: Shows 'Generating your fit...' with Sparkles animation
